name: MedicoSAM: Robust Improvement of SAM for Medical Imaging
snippet: The emergence of vision foundation models, especially Segment Anything, offers a path to universal segmentation for medical images, overcoming these issues. Here, we study how to improve Segment Anything for medical images by comparing different finetuning strategies on a large and diverse dataset.

name: How to build the best medical image segmentation algorithm
snippet: While foundation models have been useful in natural language processing and some vision tasks for some time, the foundation model developed with image segmentation in mind - Segment Anything Model (SAM) - has been developed only recently and has shown similar promise.

name: Research on Medical Image Segmentation Based on SAM and Its
snippet: While direct application of SAM to complex, multimodal, and multi-target medical datasets may not yet yield optimal results, insights from these efforts provide crucial guidance for developing foundational models tailored to the intricacies of medical image analysis.

name: A review of the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for medical
snippet: This work compares the performance of SAM, MedSAM, and SAM-Med2D with other well-established models applied to medical image segmentation, namely U-Net and nnU-Net.

name: A Review of Deep Learning Approaches Based on Segment  - MDPI
snippet: Medical image segmentation has undergone significant changes in recent years, mainly due to the development of base models. The introduction of the Segment Anything Model (SAM) represents a major shift from task-specific architectures to universal architectures.
